explanation: The requirement is not expressing a behavioral property but rather enumerating operating modes supported by the system. By mapping each operating mode to its corresponding atomic proposition, we can interpret the requirement as: 'The system implements (i.e., supports transition to and functioning in) all of the listed modes.' This is best captured by stating that eventually each mode becomes active (i.e., the system is capable of reaching each mode). Therefore, we combine all operating modes' atomic propositions (StartUpMode, SelfTestMode, StandbyMode, AlarmTestMode (see note below), PCVMode, PSVMode, failSafeMode) and require that eventually each is satisfied at some point in system execution. Since there is no behavioral or scenario ordering specified, and only mode availability is required, a simple eventuality combined with logical AND (i.e., all must be possible) suffices. Note: There is no atomic proposition directly corresponding to 'Alarm Test Mode' in the provided atomic propositions. Thus, it is omitted from the translation; the others are mapped directly.
